originalTitle: Wall Street ends higher on cool inflation data, solid bank earnings
summary: STORY: U.S. stocks closed mostly higher on Tuesday, with the Dow ending little changed, the S&P 500 adding almost four-tenths of a percent and the Nasdaq gaining nine-tenths of a percent. Second-quarter earnings season began with five big U.S. banks reporting solid results, buoyed by trading strength and dealmaking. Shares of Goldman Sachs surged 9%, while JPMorgan Chase and Bank of America also closed higher. Robert Conzo, CEO and managing director of The Wealth Alliance, said he expects corporate earnings this quarter to remain strong which should help markets advance. "When you look at the earnings estimates for the second quarter, it's at 23.6 [percent], north of 20. That's coming off of a first quarter earnings of about 27 [percent]. Again, a huge number north of 20. The last time that happened was in the first and second quarter of 2021 when we were coming off of Covid. So these are big earnings projections going forward. The market was looking for that because all eyes on earnings in the face of sticky inflation.” But inflation was a little less "sticky" in June, as Tuesday's release of the Labor Department's Consumer Price Index showed the annual inflation rate dropped to 3.5%, due largely to receding oil prices that have since rebounded. Other stocks on the move Tuesday included U.S.- listed shares of SK Hynix, which soared more than 27% after tumbling on Monday following a strong Friday debut. And shares of IBM plummeted more than 25% after the tech company warned second-quarter revenue would fall below estimates.

Second-quarter earnings season began with five big U.S. banks reporting solid results, buoyed by trading strength and dealmaking. Shares of Goldman Sachs surged 9%, while JPMorgan Chase and Bank of America also closed higher. "When you look at the earnings estimates for the second quarter, it's at 23.6 [percent], north of 20. That's coming off of a first quarter earnings of about 27 [percent]. Again, a huge number north of 20. The last time that happened was in the first and second quarter of 2021 when we were coming off of Covid. So these are big earnings projections going forward. The market was looking for that because all eyes on earnings in the face of sticky inflation.” But inflation was a little less "sticky" in June, as Tuesday's release of the Labor Department's Consumer Price Index showed the annual inflation rate dropped to 3.5%, due largely to receding oil prices that have since rebounded. Other stocks on the move Tuesday included U.S.- listed shares of SK Hynix, which soared more than 27% after tumbling on Monday following a strong Friday debut. And shares of IBM plummeted more than 25% after the tech company warned second-quarter revenue would fall below estimates.
chineseTitle: 通胀数据温和、银行盈利稳健，华尔街收高
chineseSummary: 故事：美国股市周二收盘大多走高，道琼斯指数收盘变化不大，标准普尔 500 指数上涨近十分之四，纳斯达克指数上涨十分之九。第二季度财报季拉开序幕，美国五家大型银行在交易强劲和交易的推动下公布了稳健的业绩。高盛股价飙升 9%，摩根大通和美国银行也收高。财富联盟首席执行官兼董事总经理罗伯特·康佐表示，他预计本季度企业盈利将保持强劲，这将有助于市场上涨。 “当你查看第二季度的盈利预测时，会发现它为 23.6%，高于 20。第一季度盈利约为 27%。同样，这是一个高于 20 的巨大数字。上一次发生这种情况是在 2021 年第一和第二季度，当时我们正摆脱新冠疫情。所以这些都是未来的重大盈利预测。市场正在寻找这一点，因为面对疫情，所有人都在关注盈利。粘性通胀。”但6月通胀的“粘性”有所减弱，周二美国劳工部发布的消费者物价指数显示，年通胀率降至3.5%，这主要是由于油价回落，但此后又有所反弹。周二上涨的其他股票包括在美国上市的 SK 海力士 (SK Hynix) 股票，该股在周五强势首发后，周一暴跌，随后飙升逾 27%。 IBM 警告称第二季度收入将低于预期，该公司股价暴跌超过 25%。

whyItMatters: 这条信息属于财经市场信号，适合用于观察利率、汇率、股债商品、金融机构和宏观政策变化。
